source_formulae: P0R04507: 6. The Frequency Hierarchy: Theta-Gamma Coupling and Hierarchical Predictive Coding
P0R04508: The Code: Information Processing in the Sentient Network

P0R04509: The brain's frequency architecture is not a monolithic chorus but a structured hierarchy of oscillations that enables multi-scale information integration. At the core of this architecture lies the phenomenon of theta-nested gamma oscillations, which provides a concrete mechanism for implementing the Hierarchical Predictive Coding (HPC) model.

P0R04511: Gamma oscillations (>30 Hz) represent "bottom-up" sensory evidence or prediction errors. The specific content of perception (e.g., the features of a visual object) is encoded in the firing patterns of neuronal assemblies oscillating at gamma frequencies. | Theta oscillations (4-8 Hz) represent "top-down" predictions or contextual frames generated by higher cortical areas (e.g., the hippocampus and prefrontal cortex). Each theta cycle acts as a temporal window for processing a coherent "scene" or informational chunk. | Beta oscillations (13-30 Hz) are increasingly understood to signal the "status quo" or the maintenance of the current predictive model, acting as a stabilising rhythm.

P0R04512: The nesting of gamma cycles within a larger theta cycle provides a natural syntax for the brain's code. The phase of the theta wave modulates the power of the gamma oscillations, allowing the brain to sequentially sample sensory input, compare it to predictions, and update the model. A mismatch between prediction (theta) and sensory evidence (gamma) generates a prediction error signal, which propagates up the hierarchy to refine the model.

P0R04513: This theta-gamma neural code is the fundamental mechanism by which the Psi-field's high-level predictive models are tested against incoming sensory data, allowing the organism to navigate its environment effectively. The microarchitecture of neurotransmitter systems, such as the distribution of NMDA and GABA receptors, provides the biophysical substrate for tuning and maintaining this delicate coupling.
